article_meta.title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwell as “evil” back in 2006, a now-retired cop informed FBI agents in 2019. The revelation is contained in a newly unsealed court document released Monday by the Department of Justice. CNBC reports Trump personally called the then-Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter “to tell him ‘thank goodness you’ [are] stopping [Epstein], everyone has known he’s been doing this,” Reiter told the FBI in October 2019, according to the FBI document, known as a 302. The document stated whistleblower Trump “was one of the very first people to call” the police when word spread Epstein was under investigation. This simple fact defies the president’s opponents who have since spread lies and falsehoods about his relationship with Epstein and convicted associate Maxwell. Trump said in the call he distanced himself from Epstein, according to the document, claiming he “got the hell out of there” when around Epstein with teenagers present. The outlet notes, “Reiter’s name is redacted from the 302. But the document identifies the interview subject as the person who had been Palm Beach’s police chief at the time of the department’s investigation of Epstein, who was Reiter.” Reiter told the Miami Herald, which first reported the document, Trump called him in 2006, after the police department’s probe of Epstein became publicly known. Trump told Reiter he had thrown Epstein out of his club, Mar-a-Lago, which is located in Palm Beach, the summary said. RELATED: RELEASE THE FILES! Trump Says “The Democrats Were Epstein’s Friends” “Trump told him [Reiter] people in New York knew Epstein was disgusting,” according to the FBI’s 302 summary of its interview with Reiter. “Trump said Maxwell was Epstein’s operative, ‘she is evil and to focus on her,’ ” the FBI said in the 302. The document came to light hours after Maxwell’s lawyer called on Trump to grant her executive clemency so she could speak “honestly” about what she knows. Maxwell, earlier Monday, repeatedly refused to testify to a House committee. Trump has consistently denied any wrongdoing in relation to Epstein, with whom he says he severed contact decades ago, and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein’s victims. Follow Simon Kent on Twitter: Follow @SunSimonKent or e-mail to: skent@breitbart.com
---

```json
{
  "summary": "A newly unsealed FBI document from 2019 reveals that President Donald Trump contacted Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to support the investigation into Jeffrey Epstein, calling the sex offender "disgusting" and his associate Ghislaine Maxwell "evil." According to the FBI 302 document released by the Department of Justice, Trump was among the first to call police when Epstein came under investigation. The former police chief reported that Trump stated he had distanced himself from Epstein, claiming he would leave situations where Epstein was present with teenagers. Trump also told Reiter he had banned Epstein from his Mar-a-Lago club and advised investigators to focus on Maxwell, describing her as Epstein's operative. The disclosure contradicts claims about Trump's relationship with Epstein and came the same day Maxwell's lawyer requested executive clemency. Trump has consistently denied wrongdoing and maintains he severed contact with Epstein decades ago, with no accusations from Epstein's victims.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Trump called Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to support the Epstein investigation, describing Epstein as 'disgusting' and Maxwell as 'evil'",

    "The information comes from a newly unsealed FBI 302 document from October 2019 interview with retired police chief",
    "Trump has never been accused of crimes by Epstein's victims and maintains he severed contact decades ago"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Breitbart Fact Check",
    "CNBC",
    "Miami Herald",
    "FBI documents",
    "Former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: A newly unsealed FBI document from 2019 reveals that President Donald Trump contacted Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to support the investigation into Jeffrey Epstein, calling the sex offender "disgusting" and his associate Ghislaine Maxwell "evil." According to the FBI 302 document released by the Department of Justice, Trump was among the first to call police when Epstein came under investigation. The former police chief reported that Trump stated he had distanced himself from Epstein, claiming he would leave situations where Epstein was present with teenagers. Trump also told Reiter he had banned Epstein from his Mar-a-Lago club and advised investigators to focus on Maxwell, describing her as Epstein's operative. The disclosure contradicts claims about Trump's relationship with Epstein and came the same day Maxwell's lawyer requested executive clemency. Trump has consistently denied wrongdoing and maintains he severed contact with Epstein decades ago, with no accusations from Epstein's victims.
key_facts: Trump called Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter in 2006 to support the Epstein investigation, describing Epstein as 'disgusting' and Maxwell as 'evil'
According to FBI documents, Trump was one of the first people to contact police when Epstein came under investigation
Trump told police he banned Epstein from Mar-a-Lago and would leave when Epstein was around teenagers
The information comes from a newly unsealed FBI 302 document from October 2019 interview with retired police chief
Trump has never been accused of crimes by Epstein's victims and maintains he severed contact decades ago
sources_represented: Former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter
